A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY REVEALED THAT AN EF-0 TRACKED ACROSS RURAL NORTHEAST JASPER COUNTY INTO SOUTHEAST BARTON COUNTY IN INTERMITTENT FASHION. MAXIMUM WINDS OF 85 MPH CAUSED DAMAGE TO SEVERAL TREES, ALONG WITH A BARN ROOF, AND AN IRRIGATION SYSTEM.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY REVEALED THAT AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH WINDS ESTIMATED AT 100 MPH BEGAN JUST EAST OF HIGHWAY 19 NEAR GLADDEN, MO. THE MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS 440 YARDS AND THE PATH LENGTH OF 5.84 MILES. THE TORNADO THEN CAUSED SOME UPLIFT TO A PORTION OF THE ROOF ON A HOUSE WHICH RESULTED IN THE PARTIAL FAILURE OF AN EXTERIOR WALL NEAR COUNTY ROAD 5510. THE TORNADO CONTINUED TRAVELING TOWARDS THE NORTHEAST, WHERE IT UPROOTED AND SNAPPED NUMEROUS TREES BEFORE COMPLETELY DESTROYING A BARN AND SMALL SHED AS IT CROSSED OVER HIGHWAY N SOUTHEAST OF TURTLE, MO. ADDITIONAL TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED AT THEIR TRUNKS AS THE TORNADO CROSSED COUNTY ROAD 5380. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR A TOTAL OF 10 MINUTES.
EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO A BARN, A GARAGE, AND TREES IN THE AREA.
DAMAGE TO ONE HOME, MULTIPLE OUTBUILDINGS AND TREES.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY REVEALED THAT AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH WINDS ESTIMATED AT 90 MPH OCCURRED JUST NORTHWEST OF RICHLAND, MO. THIS TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 4 MINUTES. THE MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS 100 YARDS AND THE PATH LENGTH WAS 2.1 MILES. THIS TORNADO TORE OFF THE ROOF OF ONE HOME AND ALSO DAMAGED A BARN. MULTIPLE TREES WERE UPROOTED WITH SEVERAL LARGE TREE LIMBS SNAPPED.

PICTURES AND VIDEO INDICATED HALF DOLLAR SIZE HAIL ALONG THE OZARK-DOUGLAS COUNTY LINE. TIME ESTIMATED VIA RADAR AS MULTIPLE HAIL CORES WENT OVER THIS LOCATION.

A NWS SURVEY CONFIRMED THAT AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NORTHEAST OF HIGHLANDVILLE AND CAUSED EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO THE ROOF OF AN OUTBUILDING. THE TORNADO CONTINUED EAST TO CRABAPPLE ROAD WHERE IT PUSHED A POOL HOUSE OFF OF ITS FOUNDATION AND UPROOTED TREES IN A CONVERGENT PATTERN. AFTER THE TORNADO CROSSED HIGHWAY 65, IT DESTROYED AN OUTBUILDING AND BROKE SEVERAL LARGE TREE LIMBS NEAR GOLDENROD ROAD. FINALLY, THE TORNADO UPROOTED SEVERAL TREES AT A PROPERTY ON PRAIRIE RIDGE ROAD PRIOR TO DISSIPATING. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 5 MINUTES, WITH MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WINDS OF 100 MPH, A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 100 YARDS, AND A PATH LENGTH OF 3.4 MILES.

AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 90 MPH DAMAGED OR DESTROYED NUMEROUS OUTBUILDINGS AS WELL AS SNAPPING/UPROOTING NUMEROUS TREES.
